analysis_rules: Evaluate agent responses against customer intent
Prefer official Corendon guidance when applicable

rules.description:
3. Booking, Ticketing & Payments

• Payment Verification Issues

We are seeing cases where money is deducted from a customer's account (sometimes twice) without a booking confirmation being generated. What is the exact workflow to verify the payment, issue the ticket, or process the refund?

• Name Change Fees

Customers escalate when faced with a 300–400 Euro fee for a major name correction. How should we justify this charge to the customer, and are there any exception criteria we can apply?

• Reschedule Fee Breakdowns

When customers request a detailed breakdown of rescheduling charges, what information or system view should we use to explain the exact cost breakdown to them?

Response: Booking, Ticketing & Payments

1. Payment Verification Issues

Q: We are seeing cases where money is deducted from a customer's account (sometimes twice) without a booking confirmation being generated. What is the exact workflow to verify the payment, issue the ticket, or process the refund?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents do not have access to verify payment status, duplicate transactions, ticket issuance, or refund processing.
• Collect the following details from the customer:
o Booking reference (if available)
o Passenger name
o Travel date
o Payment date and amount
o Payment method
o Transaction ID/Authorization ID
o Proof of payment (bank statement or payment receipt), if available
• Do not confirm that payment has been received or that a refund or ticket will be issued.
• Escalate the case to L3 Support for verification with the Finance/Reservations Team.
• L3 will verify whether:
o The payment was successfully received.
o A booking or ticket exists.
o A duplicate payment was processed.
o A ticket can be issued or a refund is applicable.
• Document all relevant details in the CRM before escalating.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
1. Cancellation / Refund / Reschedule Requests
Expected Agent Action:
The agent must first verify the actual booking source.
If the booking was made through a third-party travel agency, OTA, or booking partner, the agent must advise the customer to contact the respective booking partner directly for cancellation, refund, or rescheduling requests.
If the booking was made through the Corendon website or mobile application, the agent should guide the customer according to the applicable cancellation, refund, or rescheduling procedure.
QC Failure Conditions:
Agent provides cancellation/refund/reschedule assistance without verifying the booking source.
Agent gives incorrect information regarding eligibility or process.
Agent advises direct processing for third-party bookings.
Severity: Major
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
3. Reschedule Fee Breakdowns

Q: When customers request a detailed breakdown of rescheduling charges, what information should agents provide?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents do not have access to the internal fare calculation or pricing engine used to determine rescheduling costs.
• Do not estimate or manually calculate the fare difference or change fees.
• If a customer requests a detailed breakdown of the rescheduling charges, collect the booking reference and details of the requested itinerary change.
• Escalate the case to L3 Support for verification.
• L3 will review the booking, verify the applicable fare rules, airline change fee, fare difference, taxes, and any additional service charges, and provide the official breakdown to the customer.
• Record all customer requests and booking details in the CRM before escalating
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
## QA Rule – Payment Verification Issues

### Purpose

Evaluate whether the support agent correctly handled payment verification, ticket issuance, and refund-related queries according to their access level and responsibilities.

---

### Agent Access Limitations

L1 and L2 agents **do not have backend access** to:

* Verify payment status.
* Confirm duplicate transactions.
* Verify ticket issuance.
* Approve or confirm refunds.
* Access Finance or Reservation backend systems.

Only **L3 Support (Development/Finance/Reservations Team)** is authorized to perform these verifications.

---

### Required Information Gathering

Before escalating, the agent should collect the following information whenever applicable:

* Booking reference (if available)
* Passenger full name
* Travel date
* Payment date
* Payment amount
* Payment method
* Transaction ID / Authorization ID
* Proof of payment (bank statement, payment receipt, or screenshot), if available

Failure to gather essential information should be recorded as a QA observation.

---

### Correct Agent Behavior

The agent should:

* Acknowledge the customer's concern.
* Gather all relevant information.
* Explain that payment, ticket, or refund verification requires backend validation.
* Escalate the case when verification cannot be performed at their support level.
* Record all relevant details in the CRM before escalation.

---

### Escalation Process

The expected escalation flow is:

* **L1 Agent** → Escalate to **L2** if unable to resolve the issue.
* **L2 Agent** → Escalate to **L3 Support** if backend verification is required or the issue remains unresolved.
* **L3 Support (Development/Finance/Reservations Team)** is responsible for verifying:

  * Payment receipt
  * Booking existence
  * Ticket issuance
  * Duplicate transactions
  * Refund eligibility

---

### Critical Errors

Mark as a **Critical Error** if the agent, without backend verification:

* Confirms that payment has been received.
* Confirms that payment failed.
* Confirms that a duplicate payment occurred.
* Confirms that a booking exists.
* Confirms that a ticket has been issued.
* Confirms that a refund has been approved.
* Promises that a refund will be processed.
* Provides a refund processing timeframe.
* Gives any final resolution that requires backend verification.
* Assures the customer that the issue has been resolved without verification.

These actions exceed the authority of L1 and L2 agents and must be treated as misleading commitments.

---

### QA Evaluation Criteria

When reviewing the conversation, determine whether the agent:

* Gathered all necessary information before responding.
* Stayed within the permissions of their support level.
* Avoided making unverified commitments.
* Escalated the issue appropriately when unable to verify or resolve it.
* Documented the case correctly before escalation.

### QA Decision

An agent **passes** this QA check if they:

* Follow the required troubleshooting process.
* Collect the necessary customer information.
* Avoid making unsupported commitments.
* Escalate the issue according to the support hierarchy.

An agent **fails** this QA check if they:

* Skip essential information gathering.
* Provide unverified confirmations or promises.
* Fail to escalate when required.
* Resolve or close the issue without the necessary backend verification.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
• Missing Personal Items

If a customer leaves an item (e.g., laptop, jacket) on the plane and contacts Lost & Found, they are sometimes assured they will get it back, but no formal PIR or tracking number is created. How do we address and track these cases?

Response: Missing Personal Items (Left on Board)

If a customer reports that they have left a personal item (e.g., a laptop, mobile phone, jacket, passport, or other belongings) on board the aircraft, inform them that Corendon Airlines does not issue a Property Irregularity Report (PIR) or tracking number for personal items left on the aircraft, as a PIR is only applicable to checked baggage.

Advise the customer that their request will be handled through the Lost & Found process. If the item is located during the aircraft cleaning or handling process, the relevant airport's Lost & Found office or the responsible ground handling agent will contact the customer with further instructions for collection or shipment, where applicable.

If the customer states that they were assured they would receive the item back but have not received any update, advise them to follow up directly with the Lost & Found office at the arrival airport. Corendon Airlines is unable to provide a tracking number or live status updates for personal belongings left on board.

Agents should document the case in the CRM by marking it as "Personal Item Left on Board – Referred to Lost & Found" and include the item description, flight number, travel date, arrival airport, and the customer's contact details in the case notes.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

Verify against official policy

rules.description:
2. Flight Disruptions & Compensation (IRROPS)

• Delay Verification

Customers frequently escalate when we cannot verify the exact length and reason for a flight delay. What internal tool or process should we use to accurately verify delay reasons and durations?

Response:

1. Delay Verification

Q: Customers frequently escalate when we cannot verify the exact length and reason for a flight delay. What internal tool or process should we use to accurately verify delay reasons and durations?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents do not have access to operational systems to verify the exact flight delay duration or the official reason for the disruption.
• Do not speculate or communicate unverified information to the customer.
• Escalate the case to L3 Support for verification with the Operations Team/OCC.
• L3 will verify the official delay duration and reason and provide the appropriate response to the customer.
• Record all customer details, flight number, travel date, and the specific information requested before escalating.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
2. Missed Connections

Q: How should we handle customers who miss a Corendon flight because their previous (separate or connected) flight was delayed?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents should not determine liability or eligibility for rebooking or compensation.
• Collect the booking reference(s), flight details, and customer explanation.
• Escalate the case to L3 Support for verification of the booking type (separate booking or protected connection) and applicable airline policy.
• L3 will review the itinerary and provide the final resolution based on Corendon Airlines' procedures.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
3. EU261 & Hotel Accommodation

Q: Customers frequently cite EU261 laws to demand hotel stays and compensation. What are the specific guidelines and approval flows for granting or denying hotel accommodations and compensation?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents are not authorized to approve or deny hotel accommodation, reimbursement, or EU261 compensation.
• Do not promise eligibility, compensation amounts, or reimbursement.
• If the customer requests hotel accommodation, expense reimbursement, or compensation, escalate the case to L3 Support for review.
• L3 will verify the operational circumstances, determine eligibility under Corendon Airlines' policy and applicable passenger rights regulations, and provide the final response or guide the customer through the official claim process.

 
4. Tarmac Delays

Q: When a flight is stuck on the tarmac, customers often contact us stating that ground or cabin crews are not providing updates. How should we handle these live escalations and provide accurate departure estimates?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents do not have access to live operational updates or estimated departure times.
• Do not provide estimated departure times or operational information unless officially confirmed.
• Escalate the case to L3 Support for verification with the Operations Team, where applicable.
• If no official information is available, advise the customer that the flight crew and airport operations will make announcements as soon as confirmed information becomes available.
• Document the interaction in the CRM before escalating the case.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
2. Name Change Fees

Q: Customers escalate when faced with a high fee for a major name correction. How should we justify this charge, and are there any exception criteria?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents should explain that name correction or name change fees are determined according to Corendon Airlines' fare conditions and ticket rules.
• Do not negotiate, waive, or promise any reduction in the applicable fee.
• If the customer claims the correction is due to a booking error, legal name change, or another exceptional circumstance, collect all supporting documents (where applicable) and escalate the case to L3 Support.
• L3 will verify whether the request qualifies for an exception under Corendon Airlines' policy and communicate the final decision.
• Ensure all supporting documents and customer comments are attached to the CRM case before escalation.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
1. Severe Allergies

Q: What is the protocol for handling sensitive escalations where a customer was served food they are allergic to or suffered an allergic reaction onboard?

Guideline:

• Treat all allergy-related complaints as high-priority safety incidents.
• L1 and L2 agents should express empathy and collect all relevant details, including:
o Booking reference
o Flight number and travel date
o Description of the allergy
o Food or beverage consumed
o Symptoms experienced
o Whether medical assistance was provided onboard or after landing
o Any supporting medical documents (if available)
• Do not determine liability, admit fault, or promise compensation.
• Escalate the case immediately to L3 Support for review with the In-Flight Services/Safety Team.
• L3 will verify the incident, coordinate with the relevant operational departments, and provide the official response regarding the complaint and any applicable resolution.
• Document all information in the CRM before escalating.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
2. Pre-Ordered Meal Discrepancies

Q: Customers present a pre-order meal confirmation, but the cabin crew advised that no meal request was available. How should this be handled?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents should request:
o Booking reference
o Flight number and travel date
o Copy of the meal confirmation or payment receipt
o Type of meal ordered
• Do not confirm whether the meal request was correctly processed or whether compensation will be provided.
• As L1 and L2 agents do not have access to verify meal synchronization between the reservation system and onboard catering, the case must be escalated to L3 Support.
• L3 will verify whether the meal was successfully added to the booking, transmitted to the catering provider, or affected by a system synchronization issue.
• If applicable, L3 will determine whether reimbursement or any goodwill compensation is appropriate according to Corendon Airlines' policy.
• Record all supporting documents and customer comments in the CRM before escalating.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
3. Onboard Wi-Fi

Q: We need clear documentation on how the Corendon Wi-Fi facility works, including purchasing, troubleshooting, and refunding failed connections.

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents should first determine:
o Flight number and travel date
o Whether the aircraft was equipped with Wi-Fi
o Whether the customer purchased the Wi-Fi service onboard or in advance
o The nature of the issue (unable to connect, slow connection, interrupted service, payment issue, etc.)
o Any proof of purchase or payment receipt
• Provide only basic troubleshooting, such as:
o Ensuring the device is in Flight Mode with Wi-Fi enabled.
o Confirming the customer connected to the aircraft's Wi-Fi network.
o Advising the customer to reconnect or restart the device if appropriate.
• L1 and L2 agents do not have access to verify Wi-Fi service availability, technical failures, or refund eligibility.
• If the issue cannot be resolved or the customer requests a refund, escalate the case to L3 Support.
• L3 will verify the Wi-Fi service logs, confirm whether a service disruption occurred, determine refund eligibility according to Corendon Airlines' policy, and provide the final response.
• Ensure all customer details, proof of purchase, and issue description are documented in the CRM before escalating.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
5. Loyalty & Airport Operations

• Miles & Loyalty Programs

Our team has not received training on Corendon's Miles, Promo Codes, or Loyalty Program. Handling these queries currently leads to immediate escalations. Can you provide training materials or an SOP for loyalty-related issues?

• Boarding Pass Scanning Errors

When a boarding pass fails to scan at the gate, ground staff sometimes refuse to help and direct the customer to call us. What action can we take remotely to resolve a live gate-scanning issue?

Response: Membership, Loyalty Program & Promo Codes

Q: Our team has not received training on Corendon's Membership, Promo Codes, or Loyalty Program. Handling these queries currently leads to immediate escalations. Can you provide an SOP?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents must first verify the original booking source, as promotional codes, loyalty benefits, or discounts may vary depending on where the booking was made.
• Confirm whether the booking was made:
o Directly through Corendon Airlines (website, mobile app, or Customer Service).
o Through a travel agency, tour operator, OTA (Online Travel Agency), or any third-party vendor.
• Some third-party agencies and travel partners operate their own loyalty programs, promotional campaigns, voucher codes, or reward schemes, which are independent of Corendon Airlines. These offers cannot be verified or managed by L1/L2 Customer Support.
• If the promotion, voucher, or loyalty benefit was issued by a third-party booking partner, advise the customer to contact the respective booking provider, as they are responsible for validating and administering their own promotional offers.
• If the booking was made directly with Corendon Airlines and the issue relates to a Corendon promotional campaign, membership account, or promo code, collect:
o Booking reference
o Registered email address
o Promo code (if applicable)
o Screenshot of the error or promotion
o Description of the issue
• L1 and L2 agents do not have access to verify:
o Membership account status
o Promo code eligibility
o Campaign participation
o Discount application failures
• Do not manually apply promo codes, override discounts, or promise exceptions.
• Escalate eligible Corendon-related cases to L3 Support for verification. L3 will confirm the booking source, campaign eligibility, account status, and provide the appropriate resolution.
• Ensure all customer information and supporting documents are recorded in the CRM before escalating.

 
Important Guidelines for L1/L2 Agents Before Escalation

Mandatory Information Gathering

Before escalating any case to L3 Support, L1 and L2 agents must clearly understand the customer's actual issue and collect all relevant information required for investigation. The quality of the escalation depends on the accuracy and completeness of the information provided.

Agent Responsibilities

• Understand the customer's concern before initiating an escalation.
• Ask only relevant and case-specific questions required to investigate the issue.
• Verify all available details within the scope of L1/L2 support.
• Collect all mandatory information (e.g., booking reference, passenger name, flight number, travel date, etc...) based on the issue type.
• Information a clear summary of the customer's concern and all collected information in the CRM before escalating the case.

Critical Compliance
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
5. Loyalty & Airport Operations

• Miles & Loyalty Programs

Our team has not received training on Corendon's Miles, Promo Codes, or Loyalty Program. Handling these queries currently leads to immediate escalations. Can you provide training materials or an SOP for loyalty-related issues?

• Boarding Pass Scanning Errors

When a boarding pass fails to scan at the gate, ground staff sometimes refuse to help and direct the customer to call us. What action can we take remotely to resolve a live gate-scanning issue?

Response: Membership, Loyalty Program & Promo Codes

Q: Our team has not received training on Corendon's Membership, Promo Codes, or Loyalty Program. Handling these queries currently leads to immediate escalations. Can you provide an SOP?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents must first verify the original booking source, as promotional codes, loyalty benefits, or discounts may vary depending on where the booking was made.
• Confirm whether the booking was made:
o Directly through Corendon Airlines (website, mobile app, or Customer Service).
o Through a travel agency, tour operator, OTA (Online Travel Agency), or any third-party vendor.
• Some third-party agencies and travel partners operate their own loyalty programs, promotional campaigns, voucher codes, or reward schemes, which are independent of Corendon Airlines. These offers cannot be verified or managed by L1/L2 Customer Support.
• If the promotion, voucher, or loyalty benefit was issued by a third-party booking partner, advise the customer to contact the respective booking provider, as they are responsible for validating and administering their own promotional offers.
• If the booking was made directly with Corendon Airlines and the issue relates to a Corendon promotional campaign, membership account, or promo code, collect:
o Booking reference
o Registered email address
o Promo code (if applicable)
o Screenshot of the error or promotion
o Description of the issue
• L1 and L2 agents do not have access to verify:
o Membership account status
o Promo code eligibility
o Campaign participation
o Discount application failures
• Do not manually apply promo codes, override discounts, or promise exceptions.
• Escalate eligible Corendon-related cases to L3 Support for verification. L3 will confirm the booking source, campaign eligibility, account status, and provide the appropriate resolution.
• Ensure all customer information and supporting documents are recorded in the CRM before escalating.

 
Important Guidelines for L1/L2 Agents Before Escalation

Mandatory Information Gathering

Before escalating any case to L3 Support, L1 and L2 agents must clearly understand the customer's actual issue and collect all relevant information required for investigation. The quality of the escalation depends on the accuracy and completeness of the information provided.

Agent Responsibilities

• Understand the customer's concern before initiating an escalation.
• Ask only relevant and case-specific questions required to investigate the issue.
• Verify all available details within the scope of L1/L2 support.
• Collect all mandatory information (e.g., booking reference, passenger name, flight number, travel date, etc...) based on the issue type.
• Information a clear summary of the customer's concern and all collected information in the CRM before escalating the case.

Critical Compliance

• Agents must not ask customers for unnecessary or irrelevant information.
• If an L1/L2 agent requests information that is not required for handling or investigating the reported issue, the interaction should be marked as a Critical Error during QA evaluation.
• Asking irrelevant questions increases customer effort, delays resolution, and negatively impacts the customer experience.

Escalation Standard

A case should be escalated to L3 only after:

The actual issue has been clearly identified.
All mandatory information has been collected.
Relevant supporting information have been obtained (where applicable).
The CRM notes contain a complete and accurate summary of the issue.
The agent has confirmed that the request cannot be resolved within the scope of L1/L2 responsibilities.
Note: Incomplete escalations or escalations with missing mandatory information may be returned by L3 for additional details, resulting in unnecessary delays in resolution.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
• Onboard Wi-Fi

We need clear documentation on how the Corendon Wi-Fi facility works, including purchasing, troubleshooting, and refunding failed connections.

Response: In-Flight Services & Experience

1. Severe Allergies

Q: What is the protocol for handling sensitive escalations where a customer was served food they are allergic to or suffered an allergic reaction onboard?

Guideline:

• Treat all allergy-related complaints as high-priority safety incidents.
• L1 and L2 agents should express empathy and collect all relevant details, including:
o Booking reference
o Flight number and travel date
o Description of the allergy
o Food or beverage consumed
o Symptoms experienced
o Whether medical assistance was provided onboard or after landing
o Any supporting medical documents (if available)
• Do not determine liability, admit fault, or promise compensation.
• Escalate the case immediately to L3 Support for review with the In-Flight Services/Safety Team.
• L3 will verify the incident, coordinate with the relevant operational departments, and provide the official response regarding the complaint and any applicable resolution.
• Document all information in the CRM before escalating.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
3. PNR / Booking Verification Requests
Expected Agent Action:
Agents do not have access to verify PNRs, booking ownership, passenger details, or booking authenticity.
All PNR and booking verification requests must be escalated to Tier 3 Support for further verification.
The agent should clearly inform the customer that the request has been escalated for verification.
QC Failure Conditions:
Agent attempts to verify a booking without authorized access.
Agent marks the issue as resolved without Tier 3 verification.
Agent provides booking-related confirmation without validation.
Severity: Critical / Misleading Information
Critical Error Definition:
Any case where an agent resolves a PNR or booking verification request without proper Tier 3 escalation and verification will be marked as a Critical Error (Misleading Information) and may result in immediate QA failure for the interaction.
Send to all QC members
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
5. Loyalty & Airport Operations

• Miles & Loyalty Programs

Our team has not received training on Corendon's Miles, Promo Codes, or Loyalty Program. Handling these queries currently leads to immediate escalations. Can you provide training materials or an SOP for loyalty-related issues?

• Boarding Pass Scanning Errors

When a boarding pass fails to scan at the gate, ground staff sometimes refuse to help and direct the customer to call us. What action can we take remotely to resolve a live gate-scanning issue?

Response: Membership, Loyalty Program & Promo Codes

Q: Our team has not received training on Corendon's Membership, Promo Codes, or Loyalty Program. Handling these queries currently leads to immediate escalations. Can you provide an SOP?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents must first verify the original booking source, as promotional codes, loyalty benefits, or discounts may vary depending on where the booking was made.
• Confirm whether the booking was made:
o Directly through Corendon Airlines (website, mobile app, or Customer Service).
o Through a travel agency, tour operator, OTA (Online Travel Agency), or any third-party vendor.
• Some third-party agencies and travel partners operate their own loyalty programs, promotional campaigns, voucher codes, or reward schemes, which are independent of Corendon Airlines. These offers cannot be verified or managed by L1/L2 Customer Support.
• If the promotion, voucher, or loyalty benefit was issued by a third-party booking partner, advise the customer to contact the respective booking provider, as they are responsible for validating and administering their own promotional offers.
• If the booking was made directly with Corendon Airlines and the issue relates to a Corendon promotional campaign, membership account, or promo code, collect:
o Booking reference
o Registered email address
o Promo code (if applicable)
o Screenshot of the error or promotion
o Description of the issue
• L1 and L2 agents do not have access to verify:
o Membership account status
o Promo code eligibility
o Campaign participation
o Discount application failures
• Do not manually apply promo codes, override discounts, or promise exceptions.
• Escalate eligible Corendon-related cases to L3 Support for verification. L3 will confirm the booking source, campaign eligibility, account status, and provide the appropriate resolution.
• Ensure all customer information and supporting documents are recorded in the CRM before escalating.

 
Important Guidelines for L1/L2 Agents Before Escalation

Mandatory Information Gathering

Before escalating any case to L3 Support, L1 and L2 agents must clearly understand the customer's actual issue and collect all relevant information required for investigation. The quality of the escalation depends on the accuracy and completeness of the information provided.

Agent Responsibilities

• Understand the customer's concern before initiating an escalation.
• Ask only relevant and case-specific questions required to investigate the issue.
• Verify all available details within the scope of L1/L2 support.
• Collect all mandatory information (e.g., booking reference, passenger name, flight number, travel date, etc...) based on the issue type.
• Information a clear summary of the customer's concern and all collected information in the CRM before escalating the case.

Critical Compliance

• Agents must not ask customers for unnecessary or irrelevant information.
• If an L1/L2 agent requests information that is not required for handling or investigating the reported issue, the interaction should be marked as a Critical Error during QA evaluation.
• Asking irrelevant questions increases customer effort, delays resolution, and negatively impacts the customer experience.

Escalation Standard

A case should be escalated to L3 only after:

The actual issue has been clearly identified.
All mandatory information has been collected.
Relevant supporting information have been obtained (where applicable).
The CRM notes contain a complete and accurate summary of the issue.
The agent has confirmed that the request cannot be resolved within the scope of L1/L2 responsibilities.
Note: Incomplete escalations or escalations with missing mandatory information may be returned by L3 for additional details, resulting in unnecessary delays in resolution.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy
